source_definition: Concept involving ducking/diving head underneath footbag before executing trick. Ducking = head ducks under bag then trick on *other* side. Diving = head dives under then trick on *same* side.
key_finding: Source CRYSTAL CLEAR on Ducking vs Diving distinction: Ducking = other-side execution; Diving = same-side execution. Modern doctrine treats both as +1 body modifiers

source_definition: Implies the 'wrong' leg circles the bag during dexterity making move more difficult. Setting leg does the dex (instead of support leg) from cross-body set; original support leg performs delay or next component. Requires sharp hip pivot.
key_finding: Source frames paradox as adding to body category (because of hip pivot). Modern doctrine: paradox=+1 body modifier. CRITICAL: paradox-tutorial.txt's '6th ADD category' framing is reconciled here — paradox-tutorial used 6th colloquially; this source clarifies paradox is technically in body category

source_definition: Pixie = quick inside-out dexterity directly following a set from a toe stall into another move. Pixie toe stall is the simplest form. Reverse direction pixie set is called Fairy.
structural_pattern: TOE > SAME IN [DEX] (pixie) or TOE > SAME OUT [DEX] (fairy)
key_finding: Source defines pixie and fairy as DIRECTION-VARIANT pair (in vs out from toe set). User's 2026-05-19 dual identity doctrine extends this: pixie also has 2-ADD trick form. Source confirms the simplest pixie trick is 'pixie toe stall'

source_definition: Set from cross-body delay but flicked immediately under support leg. Clipper set Symposium Mirage done in front of another move. Adds a dexterity to the front of the move just as with Pixie but more difficult because you don't put your foot down and you get the symposium body add. Therefore a pogo-style move is usually worth 2 adds more than its non-pogo namesake.
source_add_contribution: (+2 via [DEX] and [BOD] flags from no-plant element)
key_finding: CRITICAL DOCTRINAL FINDING: Source says pogo adds +2 ADD ('usually worth 2 adds more than its non-pogo namesake') = +1 dex + +1 body (symposium). Modern doctrine: pogo=+0 modifier (Red pt5-locked). Reconciliation: source counts the structural contribution; modern modifier value represents only the ADDITIONAL increment beyond what's already in the operational notation (the +1 dex and +1 body are already counted via flags)

source_definition: Concept similar to pogo except the set goes under BOTH legs before executing the move.
key_finding: Modern doctrine has shooting at +3/+3 set modifier — high ADD value consistent with 'under both legs' being more difficult than pogo's single-leg under-set

source_definition: Concept involving planting both feet at once after setting the footbag then executing the desired move. Example: 'Stomping double leg-over' (Set -> Stomp -> Double leg-over).
key_finding: CRITICAL NEW FINDING: Source defines 'Stomping' as a distinct move element. Modern doctrine: NOT in modifier table; NOT in modifier_type enum. Source-only concept; surface as Wave 2

source_definition: Concept for moves where the leg performing a dexterity is planted immediately before AND after the dexterity while the other (setting) leg does not touch the ground. Requires more strength and balance since the dexterity is performed in the air.
key_finding: Source CLEAR distinction: in symposium the SETTING leg never touches ground; the DEX leg plants before+after dex. Modern doctrine: symposium=+1 body modifier
